summary: Addressed systems_architect REQUEST_CHANGES_R2 P1 blockers. Authoritative M4 march state now participates in canonical WorldState text and stateHash, preserving routeSegments as ordered path state and stable-sorting unordered sublists. Missing joinedCommitmentTroops on a march is now a structured invalid-schema validation error instead of a runtime throw. Added focused regressions for hash sensitivity and missing-ledger validation.

tests_run.result: ESLint passed for configured app, package, test, storybook, and tools source globs.
Node and worker-compatible deterministic hello, command/query, and save/load simulations matched.
Architecture boundaries passed; determinism passed; 30 root Vitest files and 202 tests passed; web Vitest 1 file and 3 tests passed.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M4-MARCH-SUPPLY-STATE-001

decisions: Added state.m4.marches to canonical WorldState text so current march position/progress, supply, arrival, route path, joined/failing reinforcements, and reason state affect stateHash.
Preserved routeSegments array order in canonical march text because it is ordered path state.
Sorted unordered march sublists in canonical text: reasonCodes, joinedCommitmentIds, joinedCommitmentTroops, failedCommitmentIds, and segment seasonRiskReasonCodes.
Made joinedCommitmentTroops required for march shape validation; malformed candidate state returns invalid-schema before runtime invariant checks.
Rainy-season pause remains documented as plan-time accepted-route season risk, not daily route reforecasting.
risks: R3 remains: authoritative WorldState hashing surface changed for m4.marches; systems_architect should review before acceptance.
Save-format remains intentionally unchanged due task boundaries; M4 march persistence should stay a separate explicit follow-up.
requested_action: Review R3 canonical march hashing and joinedCommitmentTroops validation fixes. Do not mark ACCEPTED until architecture review passes.
